name: Grafana k6
what_it_does: Open-source JavaScript-based load testing tool for APIs, microservices, and websites. Tests are written in JavaScript/TypeScript, executed as a CLI command, and can output metrics to Grafana, InfluxDB, or Grafana Cloud k6. Supports HTTP, WebSocket, gRPC, and browser testing.
use_cases: Load and stress testing REST APIs to find performance bottlenecks
Smoke, load, soak, spike, and breakpoint test scenarios
Performance regression testing in CI/CD pipelines
WebSocket and gRPC endpoint performance validation
Browser performance testing with k6 browser module
not_for: Functional UI testing (use Playwright or Cypress instead)
Teams that need a no-code load testing interface
Testing protocols beyond HTTP/WebSocket/gRPC without extensions
Real-time geographically distributed load from many regions without Grafana Cloud k6
best_when: An agent needs to programmatically generate and execute performance test scripts against HTTP APIs with a code-first approach.
avoid_when: You need a GUI-driven load testing tool or require distributed load from global regions without a cloud service.

auth.notes: CLI tool requires no auth for local use. Grafana Cloud k6 REST API uses API token authentication via Authorization: Bearer header. Tokens are scoped to Grafana Cloud stack.

pricing.free_tier_limits: OSS CLI is fully free. Grafana Cloud k6 free tier: 500 virtual user hours/month.
pricing.paid_tiers: Grafana Cloud k6 from $49/mo for additional VU hours and features
pricing.notes: The open-source CLI has no cost or usage restrictions. Grafana Cloud k6 adds hosted test runs, distributed load, and long-term metrics storage. Free tier is sufficient for moderate CI usage.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: k6 CLI output is well-structured with threshold pass/fail indicators and summary statistics. Error messages for script syntax issues are clear. HTTP response errors in test output are aggregated and may not surface individual failures clearly at scale.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Load test scripts are inherently reproducible when targeting the same endpoint. CLI execution is idempotent. Grafana Cloud k6 run IDs are unique per execution.
known_agent_gotchas: k6 scripts must use k6's module system (import/export) - standard Node.js require() is not supported
VU (virtual user) initialization code runs once per VU, not once per iteration - common agent code generation mistake
Sleep statements (k6/sleep) are required between requests to model realistic user think time; agents often omit them
k6 outputs metrics to stdout as JSON/text; parsing results requires post-processing unless using Grafana Cloud
The --out flag for multiple output destinations has changed syntax across major versions
Browser module requires Chromium installed separately and a specific test script structure

score_rationale: k6 is a strong load testing tool with a clean JavaScript API and excellent documentation. Agent-readiness is moderate: test scripts are easy to generate programmatically, the CLI is scriptable, and Grafana Cloud adds a REST API for result retrieval. Scores lower because there is no MCP server, results parsing from CLI requires additional tooling, and the JavaScript module system differences from Node.js can trip up agent-generated code. The Grafana Cloud REST API surface is not comprehensively documented compared to purpose-built API products.
